zh.Erskine, Renfrewshire, Scotland: 蘇格蘭雷福福爾厄斯金
zh.lifeSummary: Jane Hatcher of Aberdeen於1217年出生在蘇格蘭。她於1249年與John Erskine在蘇格蘭雷福福爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1252年在她的家鄉享年35歲。

zh-hans.Erskine, Renfrewshire, Scotland: 苏格兰雷福福爾厄斯金

nameMeanings.name: Hatcher
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southern): topographic name for someone who lived by a gate, from Middle English haccher(e), a derivative of hacche ‘gate’. This normally denoted a gate marking the entrance to a forest or other enclosed piece of land, sometimes a floodgate or sluice-gate. Compare Hatch .

variants.name: Hatch
meanings: English: habitational name from Hatch (Bedfordshire, Devon, Hampshire, Somerset, Wiltshire), Hacche in South Molton (Devon), or else a topographic name from residence near a hatch, a gate leading to a forest or sometimes a sluice-gate, from Middle English hacche ‘gate’ (Old English hæcce, Kentish Old English hec(c)). This name has been in Ireland since the 17th century, associated with County Meath and the nearby part of Louth.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Aberdeen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from the city of Aberdeen in northeast Scotland. The placename, which originally denoted the old city to the north of modern Aberdeen, derives from Pictish aber ‘confluence, river-mouth’ + the river name Don (named for the Celtic goddess Devona).

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: Jane Hatcher of Aberdeen was born in 1217, in Scotland. She married John Erskine in 1249, in Renfrewshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1252, in her hometown, at the age of 35.

fr.lifeSummary: Jane Hatcher of Aberdeen est née en 1217, à Écosse. Elle a épousé John Erskine en 1249, à Renfrewshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée en 1252, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 35 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Jane Hatcher of Aberdeen wurde 1217 in Scotland geboren. Sie hat 1249 in Renfrewshire, Scotland John Erskine geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1252 im Alter von 35 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Jane Hatcher of Aberdeen è nata il nel 1217 nel Scotland. Lei sposato John Erskine nel 1249, nel Renfrewshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1252, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 35 anni.

ja.Erskine, Renfrewshire, Scotland: スコットランドレンフリューシュアイアエルスキン
ja.Renfrewshire, Scotland: スコットランドレンフリューシュアイア
ja.lifeSummary: Jane Hatcher of Aberdeenは1217年にスコットランドで生まれた。1249年にスコットランドレンフリューシュアイアでJohn Erskineと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1252年に故郷で35歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Hatcher of Aberdeen(은)는 1217년 스컷랜드에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1249년 스컷랜드 렌프레w실르에서 John Erskine(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1252년 그녀의 고향에서 35세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Jane Hatcher of Aberdeen nasceu em 1217, em Scotland. Ela casou-se com John Erskine em 1249, em Renfrewshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 1252, em sua cidade natal, com 35 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Jane Hatcher of Aberdeen родилась в 1217 г. в местности Шотландия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Erskine в 1249 г. в местности ренфрюшир, Шотландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1252 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 35 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Jane Hatcher of Aberdeen nació en 1217, en Scotland. Se casó con John Erskine en 1249, en Renfrewshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1252, en su pueblo, a la edad de 35 años.
